title: Something Happened
description: You witness an accident near your flat and need to call for help — then tell Marek what happened.
context: You are walking near your flat when you see someone fall and get hurt. You call for help, then later tell Marek what happened.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Tiesňová linka, dobrý deň. Čo sa stalo?
exchanges.translation: Emergency line, good day. What happened?
exchanges.prompt: The dispatcher answers. Someone fell and is injured. What do you say first?

choices.translation: Someone is injured! Send an ambulance!

exchanges.correctFeedback: Niekto je zranený! Pošlite záchranku! — exactly right. State the emergency immediately and clearly.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Niekto je zranený! Pošlite záchranku! is the correct phrase for a medical emergency — say it immediately.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Rozumiem. Volám zdravotnícku pomoc. Aká je vaša adresa?
exchanges.translation: I understand. I'm sending medical help. What is your address?
exchanges.prompt: The dispatcher asks for your address. Pick whichever address detail you'd give first.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Moja adresa je... / Bývam na ulici... — correct pattern. Any address works, the grammar is what matters.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Moja adresa je... or Bývam na ulici... + street + number is the pattern for giving your address.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.translation: Thank you. Are you inside or outside?
exchanges.prompt: You are standing outside on the street. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Som vonku. — correct. Vonku = outside, exactly where you are.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som vonku is correct since you're outside on the street, not inside a building.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Dobre. Je to blízko vašej budovy?
exchanges.translation: Good. Is it near your building?
exchanges.prompt: The accident happened right in front of your building. What do you say?

choices.translation: I am in front of the building.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Som pred budovou. — correct and precise. Pred = in front of, exactly where you are standing.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som pred budovou (I am in front of the building) gives the dispatcher a precise location.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Výborne, presná lokalizácia pomáha. Je niečo iné nablízku, ako obchod alebo lekáreň?
exchanges.translation: Excellent, a precise location helps. Is there anything else nearby, like a shop or pharmacy?
exchanges.prompt: There is a pharmacy right next to your building. What do you say?

choices.translation: I am next to the pharmacy.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Som vedľa lekárne. — correct. Vedľa = next to, an extra landmark that helps the ambulance find you faster.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som vedľa lekárne (I am next to the pharmacy) is correct — it gives a precise extra landmark.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Záchranka je na ceste. Bude tam o desiatej minúte. Zostanete s ním?
exchanges.translation: The ambulance is on its way. It will be there in ten minutes. Will you stay with him?
exchanges.prompt: You agree to stay with the injured person. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, zostanem s ním. — correct. Zostanem = I will stay, from the Verbs lesson, used exactly as promised in real life.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Áno, zostanem s ním (Yes, I'll stay with him) is the responsible and correct response here.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Ahoj! Počul som sirény pri tvojej budove. Čo sa stalo?
exchanges.translation: Hi! I heard sirens near your building. What happened?
exchanges.prompt: Marek calls later asking what happened. How do you start explaining?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Niekto spadol a bol zranený. — correct, matches what actually happened.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Niekto spadol a bol zranený (Someone fell and was injured) correctly describes the situation.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: To je hrozné. Kde presne sa to stalo?
exchanges.translation: That's terrible. Where exactly did it happen?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks exactly where it happened. What do you say?

choices.translation: In front of the building, next to the pharmacy.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Pred budovou, vedľa lekárne. — correct, exactly matching what you told the dispatcher.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Pred budovou, vedľa lekárne (in front of the building, next to the pharmacy) matches the actual location.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: A zostal si s ním, kým prišla záchranka?
exchanges.translation: And did you stay with him until the ambulance arrived?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you stayed with the injured person. You did. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, zostal som s ním. — correct, confirming you kept your word and stayed.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Áno, zostal som s ním (Yes, I stayed with him) confirms what you promised the dispatcher.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Si dobrý človek. Som rád, že je v poriadku. Potrebuješ niečo?
exchanges.translation: You're a good person. I'm glad he's okay. Do you need anything?
exchanges.prompt: Marek offers help. You're fine but appreciate his concern. What do you say?

choices.text: Som v poriadku, ďakujem za starostlivosť.
choices.translation: I'm fine, thank you for caring.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Som v poriadku, ďakujem za starostlivosť. — warm and complete. You reassured him and thanked him.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som v poriadku, ďakujem za starostlivosť (I'm fine, thank you for caring) is the warm, natural close to this conversation.

completionMessage: 🎉 Well handled! You gave your address, described your exact position, called for help, and explained the whole situation to Marek afterward — all in Slovak.
